hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic substitutions at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3 and TM5/TM6, while avoiding off-target contacts with SSTR3/SSTR5. This will increase the selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 affinity and reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that SSTR2 selectivity can be improved by disrupting conserved pocket interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 at non-pharmacophore positions. The next iteration will test this by introducing mutations at positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve SSTR2 selectivity, we must disrupt conserved pocket interactions in off-targets SSTR3 and SSTR5. These positions are non-pharmacophore and can be mutated without affecting the core binding motif (FWKT7-10) or disulfide bonds (Cys3/Cys14).

These mutations are designed to break hydrogen bonds and hydrophobic interactions in the conserved pockets of SSTR3/SSTR5, which are likely responsible for off-target binding. They are conservative or neutral in nature and should not interfere with SSTR2 binding.
